response.justification: Mohammed Al-Said's profile is fundamentally misaligned with this role. His C++ experience is marginal—limited to roughly six months at National Instruments writing components for a C IDE plus an undergraduate thesis project—far short of the mandatory 5–10 years of hands-on Windows C++ development. His core expertise is in TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang for web and distributed systems. There is no Fixed Income, Fixed Income Derivatives, Investment Banking, or Capital Markets domain exposure anywhere in 15+ years of employment history. SQL Server and Oracle are absent from the resume. Strong engineering fundamentals and CI/CD discipline do not offset these missing mandatory qualifications.
strengths: Broad polyglot engineering background with demonstrated ability to pick up new languages and stacks rapidly
Distributed systems and high-throughput experience at Dunnhumby, handling millions of HTTP requests per second with latency-sensitive design
Strong communication and knowledge-sharing track record via conference talks, workshops, and active open-source leadership
concerns: C++ experience is minimal (~6 months at National Instruments plus a thesis project), nowhere near the mandatory 5–10 years required
Zero Fixed Income, Capital Markets, or Investment Banking domain exposure across the entire employment history
No evidence of Windows-platform C++ development, SQL Server usage, or Oracle experience—all of which are mandatory requirements
